quoteFirst: and they found that they had nothing more to say to each other, except that the weather was fine and that it was five or
quoteLast: , or it might be ten past, because the castle clock was always slow.

quoteFirst: Starting down the river to fish soon after breakfast, he became so engrossed in his sport that he forgot all about lunch, and did not return till dinner-time, when he walked abstractedly into the room where we were sitting, and pulled out his watch; then after studying it and making calculations for a short time he remarked slowly, ‘I left here at
quoteLast: , and hanged if it isn’t ten minutes past six now; my watch must have stopped.’
title: Three in Norway, by Two of Them
